name: Lamb Sans Mint
recipeIngredient: 1 leg of lamb, butterflied and trimmed
recipeInstructions:
1. Carefully trim the fat from a butterflied leg of lamb and finish the cut as needed so that the lamb lies flat in a butterfly shape. A large butterflied leg of lamb should be no more than 6 to 8 cm thick.
2. Crush the rosemary and rub it into the lamb. Sprinkle black pepper on the cut side of the meat.
3. Combine all remaining ingredients (wine, green onions, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, thyme, and onion) in a marinade. Place the lamb in a shallow dish or bowl, cover as much as possible with the marinade, and refrigerate, covered, for 4 to 24 hours. Turn at intervals to ensure even marination.
4. Grill over mesquite and charcoal on a grill with a tight-fitting lid (e.g., Weber). Cook one side for 25 minutes, occasionally spooning some leftover marinade onto the uncooked side, then turn and cook an additional 20 minutes. Check doneness by making a small incision in the thickest part of the lamb; it should be still pink but not raw. Remove from grill and prepare to serve. The lamb will continue to cook slightly after removal.
5. Serve the lamb cut-side down (pretty side up). Garnish with uncooked green onions.
Difficulty: Easy if you have a good butcher.
